name: blogbuddy
description: Your AI-powered blog writing sidekick that makes Markdown magic happen!

blogbuddy.apiKey.markdownDescription:
API key. **Resolution order** (first non-empty wins):
1. This setting
2. `$BLOGBUDDY_API_KEY` environment variable
3. `$OPENAI_API_KEY` environment variable

Leave empty to rely on the env vars. Run [BlogBuddy: Show Config Diagnostics](command:blogbuddy.showDiagnostics) to see which source is currently in use.

blogbuddy.baseURL.markdownDescription:
OpenAI-compatible base URL (e.g. `https://api.openai.com/v1`, `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`). **Resolution order** (first non-empty wins):
1. This setting
2. `$BLOGBUDDY_BASE_URL` environment variable
3. `$OPENAI_BASE_URL` environment variable
4. Default: `https://api.openai.com/v1`

Leave empty to use the env vars or the OpenAI official endpoint. Run [BlogBuddy: Show Config Diagnostics](command:blogbuddy.showDiagnostics) to verify.

blogbuddy.model.markdownDescription:
Model id used for all BB commands (e.g. `gpt-4o-mini`, `openai/gpt-4o-mini` on OpenRouter).

**Recommended:** click [BlogBuddy: Select Model](command:blogbuddy.selectModel) to open a picker populated from the provider's model list (auto-fetched from the configured base URL).

blogbuddy.assetDir.description: Relative subdirectory for uploaded assets in BB Editor (e.g. "assets" or "images/uploads"). Resolved from the document directory. Leave empty to save alongside the document.

blogbuddy.reader.inlineAssets.description: When exporting from BB Reader, inline local images as base64 to produce a single self-contained HTML file. Set false to keep relative image paths.

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
